description:
bl review fails on bare-repo layouts: it runs `git merge --squash` from store.root, but when store.root is a bare repo (e.g. a `.git` with `core.bare=true` and worktrees attached) git refuses with `this operation must be run in a work tree`.

Strace confirms bl chdirs to the bare repo and runs the squash there with no GIT_WORK_TREE override.

Fix: detect bare repos and route the squash through an ephemeral detached worktree (`git worktree add --detach`), then update refs/heads/<main> from the bare repo. Non-bare layouts keep the existing direct-squash code path.

Reasoning: the user's layout (bare .git with worktrees) is a legitimate setup — the `worktree_dir` config option implies it's designed-for. The bug is intermittent because it succeeds whenever the squash happens to run somewhere with a working tree; users hit this once, assume flake, and re-run, never investigating.
title: review: route squash through worktree when repo root is bare
